evidences.title: World Cup Format and Rules 2026 Explained for India
evidences.content: ## How Does the World Cup Format Work? The World Cup format starts with a group stage and then moves into knockout matches. In the 2026 tournament, official organisers have confirmed a 48-team format with 12 groups of four teams before the best-performing sides advance into the Round of 32. Each team begins in the group stage, where they play three matches against the other teams in their group. Teams earn points based on results, and the strongest teams move into the knockout stage. After the group stage, the tournament moves into single-elimination football. Teams that lose a knockout match are eliminated, while winners continue progressing toward the final. The 2026 World Cup includes: [...] In the 2026 World Cup format, each group contains four teams. Every team plays the other teams in its group once, meaning each nation plays three group matches. After all group matches are completed, standings are determined by points. The top two teams in each group qualify automatically for the knockout rounds. The new 48-team format also allows the eight best third-placed teams to qualify for the Round of 32. This means some teams can still advance even if they finish third in their gr...

evidences.content: Mexico City is the only capital of the three host nations chosen as a venue site; Ottawa and Washington, D.C., joined Bonn (West Germany, 1974) and Tokyo (Japan, 2002) as the only capital cities not selected to host World Cup matches. Washington was a host city candidate, but the poor state of Northwest Stadium caused the city to combine its bid with nearby Baltimore's M&T Bank Stadium, which was unsuccessful. Other cities eliminated from the final hosting list were Cincinnati, Denver, Nashville, Orlando, and Edmonton. Ottawa's candidate venue, TD Place Stadium, was eliminated early on for insufficient capacity. evidences.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Travel Guide: Transport, Visa, Things to Do & Practical Tips
evidences.content: Show more The 2026 FIFA World Cup is one of the biggest football events in the world — and the 2026 edition is especially important for travellers because it is hosted across three countries. Here is what fans need to know before planning a trip: What it is: the FIFA World Cup is an international football tournament where national teams compete for the world title. When it takes place: the 2026 tournament runs from June to July 2026. Who hosts it: the tournament is hosted by the USA, Canada and Mexico. How big it is: the 2026 edition features 48 teams and 104 matches across 16 host cities. Why travel planning is different: fans may need to plan not only match tickets, but also visas, border crossings, flights between cities, local transport, hotels and payment methods. [...] World Cup 2026 is also a chance to explore the host cities beyond the stadium. If your schedule allows, add at least one extra day before or after the match for sightseeing, food, fan events or a sports bar experience. ## A practical plan is simple: on matchday: choose one nearby attraction, then leave early for the stadium; on non-match days: visit major landmarks, museums or neighbourhoods; without a stad...

evidences.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Complete Guide to the Tournament - Britannica
evidences.content: A majority of the 2026 World Cup matches are taking place in the United States, with 11 U.S. cities serving as hosts: Los Angeles, Miami, Atlanta, Seattle, Houston, Philadelphia, Kansas City, Boston, Dallas, the San Francisco Bay Area, and the New York Citymetropolitan area. The host cities in Mexico are Guadalajara, Mexico City, and Monterrey; and the host cities in Canada are Toronto and Vancouver. The World Cup previously took place in North America three times: 1970 and 1986 in Mexico, and in 1994 in the United States. The 2026 tournament is the first time the men’s World Cup has been played in Canada. FIFA, Football, and the World Cup [...] The 2026 FIFA World Cup, an international men’s football (soccer) tournament organized by the Fédération Internationale de Football Association (FIFA; world governing body of association football), is taking place in North America. Matches are being held in 16 cities across the United States, Mexico, and Canada. The 2026 FIFA World Cup began on June 11 and will end on July 19 after a total of 104 matches (72 in the group stage and 32 in the knockout stage). It is the first time that three countries are jointly hosting a World Cup and onl...

evidences.content: In the US, matches will be hosted in Atlanta, Boston, Dallas, Houston, Kansas City, Los Angeles, Miami, New York/New Jersey, Philadelphia, Seattle and the San Francisco Bay Area. Expect other cities and popular tourist destinations across the US to be busier than usual during the tournament. ## Visa and entry requirements Check the entry requirements for the US, especially information on passport validity, visa requirements and applying for an Electronic System for Travel Authorization (ESTA). If you have purchased FIFA World Cup 2026 tickets directly from FIFA, you can make a US visa appointment through FIFA Priority Appointment Scheduling System (PASS).

Game Tickets Skills Tickets Major League Soccer # FIFA World Cup 2026 format explained MLSsoccer staff Share on Facebook Share on X The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be co-hosted by the United States, Canada and Mexico – bringing together 48 nations for the world's preeminent soccer tournament. Here's how the format will play out: ### What is the group stage format? 48 teams divided into 12 groups of four Each team plays three group opponents in a round-robin style The top two teams in each group advance to the Round of 32 The eight third-place teams across the 12 groups with the most points also advance to the Round of 32 ### How do knockout rounds work? [...] ### How do knockout rounds work? Beginning in the Round of 32, the tournament will proceed with single-elimination matches culminating in the tournament final on Sunday, July 19. From this stage onward, the tournament will feature single-elimination, playoff matches. Matches consist of two 45-minute halves in regulation. If still tied after regulation, the match will go to extra time, which consists of two 15-min...

evidences.title: FIFA World Cup - Wikipedia
evidences.content: The final tournament format from 1998 until 2022 had 32 national teams competing over the course of a month in the host nations, before it was expanded to 48 teams beginning with the 2026 World Cup. There are two stages: the group stage, followed by the knockout stage. In the group stage, teams compete within twelve groups of four teams each. Twelve teams are seeded, including the hosts, with the other seeded teams selected using a formula based on the FIFA World Rankings or performances in recent World Cups, and drawn to separate groups. The other teams are assigned to different "pots", usually based on geographical criteria, and teams in each pot are drawn at random to the eight groups. Since 1998, constraints have been applied to the draw to ensure that no group contains more than two [...] 77. ↑ Slater, Matt; Ornstein, David (14 March 2023). evidences.title: Turkey vs United States Prediction: World Cup 2026 Preview & Best Bets | TFA
evidences.content: The United States’ two World Cup wins have come against competitive opponents. A 2-0 clean sheet against Australia and a 4-1 demolition of Paraguay demonstrate an attack that has found its rhythm and a defence that has been solid when it matters. Their two pre-tournament defeats came against Germany and Portugal, which gives those results appropriate context. ## Turkey vs United States Head to Head These two sides have met five times in total. The record is evenly balanced, with two wins each and one draw. The most recent meeting, a June 2025 friendly, saw Turkey win 2-1 on United States soil, which is a useful data point as they return to American soil for a competitive setting. [...] Earlier meetings saw the United States win 2-1 in friendlies in June 2014 and May 2010. Turkey beat the United States 2-1 in the 2003 Confederations Cup, while the first known meeting ended 1-1 in September 1991. The Turkey vs United States head-to-head record gives Turkey supporters at least some historical encouragement, particularly given that 2025 victory. However, the context of a must-win World Cup group game on home soil for the United States makes that precedent a fragile foundation for a...

evidences.title: How the FIFA World Cup 26™ will work with 48 teams
evidences.content: Close Related Content Close Share The tournament will be the first ever to feature 48 teams. For this reason, the 32-team, eight-group, format has been replaced by one with 12 groups of four. The top two teams in each group, plus the eight best third-placed teams, will advance to the round of 32. Adopting 12 groups of four was chosen over the option of 16 groups of three teams, based on a thorough review that took into consideration a number of factors, including team and fan experience, player welfare and sporting integrity. The new format means the team that triumphs in the final on 19 July will have played eight games to be crowned champions, one more than at Qatar 2022, after the inclusion of an additional round of 32 knockout phase. evidences.title: Every New 2026 World Cup Rule Explained in 9 minutes
evidences.content: throw-in to run the clock down. Instead of gaining a few extra seconds, they risk losing possession completely. Goal kick rule. A goal kick happens when the attacking team puts the ball over the goal line without scoring, usually from a missed shot or bad cross. Goalkeepers used to be able to place the ball, walk away, come back, check their gloves, look at the pitch, shout instructions, and generally take as long as they wanted before restarting play. Now, goal kicks must be taken without unnecessary delay. If a team drags it out too much, the opponents are awarded a corner kick. That is a much stronger punishment than just a warning because a corner kick is a real attacking opportunity. For beginners, corners are dangerous because the attacking team can put the ball straight into the [...] arguing with opponents or officials, making it impossible to know what was actually being said. Now, doing this during a confrontation can result in a red card. This is about transparency. If players are going to argue on the pitch, officials and [music] cameras should be able to see what's being said, partly to prevent abusive language going [music] unchecked. Protest walk-off rule. Players...

evidences.title: How FIFA World Cup rules on extra time and tiebreakers ...
evidences.content: The golden goal was abolished in 2004 by the International Football Association Board after negative feedback from coaches, referees and players. According to some analyses, the rule led to more defensive and cautious play from players too afraid to concede the game-winning goal, despite the intention of the rule to encourage more exciting play in extra time. ## New rules added for the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup The IFAB approved new rules for the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup, including expanding a countdown rule to apply to throw-ins and goal kicks to help speed up the game. If the referee considers that a throw-in or goal kick is taking too long or is being deliberately delayed, the referee can initiate a five‑second visual countdown. [...] ## Added time, extra time and tiebreaker rules Each match is 90 minutes, split into two 45-minute halves. At the end of each half, however, there will be added time to make up for stoppages in play — such as when a player is injured, or there is a lengthy replay review — when the clock keeps running. If a match is still tied at the end of regulation, it goes into extra time with 30 minutes added. This time will be divided into two 15-minute per...

evidences.title: USMNT vs. Turkey World Cup mega-preview - ny times
evidences.content: It’s difficult to test the motivation of either team when both have little to gain, although perhaps Turkey would like to return home with something and a win or draw could salvage some pride. A Turkey win is around +250 (5-to-2) while a draw is +300 (3-to-1). That means the implied chance of a U.S. win is about 50 percent while a draw and a Turkey win split the remaining probability mostly evenly. Keep in mind, American bettors tend to bet on America, of course, but the sportsbooks won’t move their lines too far to compensate for that.
